author: Sharbel Marshi
description: Embed websites and automatically fill URLs and login credentials when you open them. - This plugin has not been manually reviewed by Obsidian staff.

manifest.author: Sharbel Marshi
manifest.description: Embed saved websites and open them in a tab, sidebar, or floating window. Includes DOM-based automatic sign-in and quick opening from notes, the ribbon, or commands.
manifest.name: Extended Browser

name: Extended Browser

releases.author: SharbelMarshi
releases.description:
This release turns Extended Browser into a more polished and safer embedded-browser workflow.

The main addition is Markdown website embeds using fenced emb and eb blocks. These blocks render websites directly inside notes through the plugin’s controlled frame logic instead of relying on arbitrary HTML. The embed UI was also refined so embedded sites appear as rounded cards rather than raw blocks.

This update also adds better engine control. Site views can use webview, embedded Markdown blocks are forced to iframe, and separate windows can safely fall back when needed. A new per-site Render engine setting is available with Auto (safe), Webview, and Iframe.

Several fixes were made to avoid the crash and was narrowed down to Electron/Chromium webview behavior rather than the plugin’s own logic, so the rendering path was adjusted accordingly. Auto-fill was also improved for React-style login fields, with better support for re-rendered inputs and iframe fallback submission.

The in-tab controls were redesigned as well. The old centered toolbar was replaced with a small hidden center notch that appears on hover, giving quick access to Back, Home, and Reload without permanently taking up space.

releases.author: SharbelMarshi
releases.description:
This release focuses on stability, login reliability, temporary browsing, and cleaning up older experimental behavior.

Removed the old gate markdown block

The old markdown ```gate embed feature caused crash inside Electron’s native rendering layer so it has been discontinued. Please use the dedicated webview mode or the temporary site command instead.

Recommended alternatives:

The dedicated webview mode works properly with Obsidian’s “Move to new window” action. You can also set the default open mode to window or tab.

For temporary browsing without saving the site, use the Command Palette command: Open Temp site

The floating window is also available for cases where you want the browser to stay above your current workspace. Unlike Obsidian’s detached window behavior, the floating window is draggable and resizable, so it can effectively be expanded to full-window size while still staying useful on top of your current note.

Temp site improvements

The temporary site feature has been cleaned up and now works as a real one-off browsing option.

Previously, temp-gate was being registered like a normal saved gate, which created a misleading command that opened an empty earth-icon tab. This has been fixed.

Changes include:

* Added a dedicated Open Temp site command
* Added a small URL modal for temporary browsing
* Temp site now opens a one-off URL without saving it
* Prevented Temp site from being registered like a normal saved gate

Login and auto sign-in improvements

This release improves login detection and automatic sign-in behavior across more websites.

Changes include:

* Strengthened input value injection with verification and retry logic
* Fixed username field detection when the username field appears before the password field
* Reset the auto-sign-in attempt state on reload/home so injection can run again when returning to login pages

Tab navigation UI improvements: tabs now include an in-view navigation toolbar which includes: * Back * Home * Reload. This makes navigation easier and avoids relying only on Obsidian’s tab header behavior.

TypeScript and project cleanup

Several TypeScript/editor issues were also fixed:

* Marked settings and floatingPreview in src/main.ts with definite assignment
* Fixed the setupInsertLinkMenu(this) type mismatch by updating the helper signature to accept Plugin
* Normalized baseUrl in tsconfig.json
* Kept moduleResolution as node for compatibility with the current TypeScript version

releases.author: SharbelMarshi
releases.description:
This release reworks Extended Browser from a site-specific login helper into a more flexible DOM-driven login system.

The plugin now scans the live page DOM to detect visible login fields instead of relying on hardcoded selector assumptions. It scores fields using labels, placeholders, autocomplete, ARIA text, field order, form proximity, and surrounding context. This makes login detection more reliable across different websites and frontend frameworks.

This release also adds support for both two-field and three-field sign-in flows. In addition to the usual email/username and password fields, the plugin can now handle login pages that require a separate identifier field, such as an ID number plus email/username plus password.

Submission handling was rebuilt as well. The plugin now looks for likely login actions across native submit buttons, custom type="button" elements, role="button" elements, and app-specific markers such as data-testid="submit-button". The submit flow now prefers custom app buttons before falling back to native form submission, click-based submission, and Enter-style submission. This was tuned against real login pages, including TAU and Brevo.

Field injection was improved for modern controlled inputs. The plugin now uses the native input setter and dispatches a more realistic sequence of input/change events around value updates, especially for password fields. This should improve compatibility with React, Next.js, and similar frameworks where simply setting .value is not enough.

Favicons were also improved. Instead of depending on a single favicon source, the plugin now normalizes URLs, tries multiple favicon sources, and falls back to a generated monogram when external icon loading fails. This avoids the generic earth icon appearing too often.

The README, package metadata, and manifest description were updated to match the new behavior.
